tasks.title: BofA/SBA Delinquency Response
tasks.what: Draft and send response letter to BofA SBA loan delinquency inquiry from Rosamelia O. Semino.
how: Pull the original BofA email from Ibrahim's inbox.
Draft a letter explaining cash timing — MTD $145K, receivables in pipeline.
Ibrahim reviews and signs the letter.
Send certified mail + email to Rosamelia O. Semino at BofA.
tasks.why: SBA loan default = credit risk, facility liens, reputational damage. Must be answered within 10 days of inquiry. Failure to respond could trigger acceleration clause.

tasks.title: PCC API Next Gate
tasks.what: Schedule App Validation meeting with PointClickCare to advance from Workflow Review PASSED to OAuth/mTLS.
how: Email PCC developer portal contact to request App Validation meeting.
Request App Validation meeting slot.
Have Chris Jagernath join — he owns the technical integration.
Confirm 3-legged OAuth scope list before the call.
tasks.why: PCC API = QIS rounding system + CCM billing automation. Worth $67–115K/month per facility in uncaptured revenue. One meeting unlocks the next gate.

tasks.title: Elkton Medical Director Contract
tasks.what: Get St. Elizabeth Medical Director contract from Google Drive and adapt it for Elkton (Luis Navas-Migueloa).
how: Ask Stacey to locate the St. Elizabeth contract in Google Drive.
Stacey sends it to Ibrahim for review.
Adapt key terms: all-clinical-services scope, same forced-payout clause.
Return adapted contract to Luis Navas-Migueloa.
tasks.why: Elkton is consolidating physicians — Ibrahim is being brought IN. This is a new revenue contract, not a termination. Delay = risk of losing the slot.

tasks.title: MTD Revenue Gap Analysis
tasks.what: Identify the root cause of the −9.3% YoY revenue gap ($145K vs $153K SPLY).
how: Pull A/R aging report from QuickBooks Online.
Identify top 3 delayed payers by dollar amount.
Check CCM billing capture rate per facility.
Compare prior month billing velocity to spot the slowdown point.
tasks.why: Gap is shrinking (was −42% in early June) but still negative. Closing it recovers $8–10K/month. Root cause analysis prevents it from widening again.

tasks.title: AccentCare DocuSign
tasks.what: Sign CTI + IPOC hospice clinical documents sent by AccentCare (Lewis, Rhona) — 2nd reminder received.
how: Check Ibrahim's Gmail for DocuSign email from AccentCare dated June 14.
Review and sign each page of the CTI and IPOC documents.
Confirm receipt with AccentCare contact.
tasks.why: Clinical documentation compliance. Patient care coordination is blocked until signed. Second reminder received — this is overdue.

tasks.title: HCSA Resubmission — Section B
tasks.what: Irma must provide QLSS's dedicated phone number to Stacey so Section B of the HCSA application can be completed.
how: Text or call Irma Santos NOW — this is the single blocking item.
Get the QLSS direct phone number from Irma.
Stacey fills Section B fields with the number.
Submit resubmission packet to Glenda Roberts at staffing.agencies@maryland.gov by June 23 (1-week buffer before June 30 hard deadline).
tasks.why: June 30 is the HARD HCSA deadline. Miss it = full restart + months of delay + $0 QLSS revenue continues. QLSS is the primary lever against the Apex revenue gap.

tasks.title: HCSA P&P Manual Rewrite
tasks.what: Rewrite QLSS Policies & Procedures manual as an operational narrative (not compliance theater).
how: Stacey drafts Who/What/When/Where per job title for every procedure.
Ibrahim reviews for clinical accuracy and completeness.
Include: intake, scheduling, placement, supervision, complaint, documentation SOPs.
Submit with Section B by June 23.
tasks.why: Previous P&P was rejected as too generic. A genuine operational narrative survives HCSA inspection. A paper shell creates survey liability later and risks rejection again.

tasks.title: Irma Santos Credentialing Packet
tasks.what: Review and respond to Irma Santos credentialing packet share request.
how: Check email labeled 'Reply Today' from Irma Santos.
Review the credentialing packet contents.
Respond with any missing items or confirmations needed.
tasks.why: Irma is the QLSS coordinator — her credentialing status affects HCSA submission and staffing operations. Delays here cascade to the June 30 deadline.

tasks.title: Malpractice Coverage Confirmation
tasks.what: Confirm malpractice policy covers IV infusion therapy AND peptide prescribing in Maryland.
how: Call current malpractice carrier — ask specifically about infusion + peptide scope in Maryland.
Request written confirmation of infusion + peptide scope in writing (email OK).
If not covered, get quotes from NORCAL, The Doctors Company, Coverys.
Mahreen tracks status and follows up if no response in 24 hours.
tasks.why: No malpractice confirmation = no clinical launch. This is a hard legal gate. Every day of delay = day of zero Maison Vitalis revenue.

tasks.title: RN Scope Confirmation
tasks.what: Confirm Maryland RN can administer IV therapy in a physician-office setting without additional certification.
how: Call Maryland Board of Nursing at 410-585-1900.
Ask specifically: 'Can an RN administer IV infusions in a physician-owned outpatient clinic under physician supervision?'
Request written or email confirmation of the ruling.
Document and file the response.
tasks.why: RN scope defines the entire staffing model and clinical workflow. A wrong assumption = liability. This call takes 15 minutes and removes a major unknown.

tasks.title: Patient Consent Forms
tasks.what: Draft and finalize patient consent forms for IV therapy and peptide treatment.
how: Use template from SSRP conference contacts or request from malpractice carrier.
Have attorney review consent form language.
Load finalized forms into EMR intake workflow.
tasks.why: Required before first patient is seen. No consent form = no treatment. Attorney review protects against liability claims.

tasks.title: IV Suite / Infusion Vendor Lock
tasks.what: Select and contract with a compounding pharmacy for IV NAD+, infusion bags, and peptide supply.
how: Contact Empower Pharmacy, Belmar Pharmacy, and 5280 Pharmacy for 503B quotes.
Get formulary lists from each vendor.
Compare turnaround time + pricing across the three.
Execute physician onboarding agreement with selected vendor.
tasks.why: No pharmacy contract = no inventory = no revenue on day 1. 503B compounding pharmacy is the only compliant source for IV compounds.

tasks.title: Maryland Business License
tasks.what: File Maryland business license and medical practice registration for Maison Vitalis.
how: File with SDAT at sdat.dat.maryland.gov.
Register medical practice with Maryland Board of Physicians.
Get EIN if not already assigned (IRS.gov online form, same-day).
tasks.why: Required before billing any patient. Confirmed: NO OHCQ facility license needed (physician office class). Mahreen can handle filing.

tasks.title: Stripe Descriptor Fix
tasks.what: Update Stripe payment statement descriptor — currently flagged as 'PHYSICIAN', Stripe requires a change.
how: Login to Stripe Dashboard.
Go to Settings → Business Settings → Statement Descriptor.
Change to 'MAISON VITALIS' or 'MV WELLNESS'.
Save and confirm the change.
tasks.why: Stripe has flagged this 3 times. Unresolved = payment processing risk and potential account hold. Five-minute fix.

tasks.title: DigiCert OV Validation
tasks.what: Follow up with DigiCert (Ridhaa at validation@digicert.com) on Apex Healthcare LLC OV validation for X9 PKI cert.
how: Reply to Ridhaa with SDAT link confirming Apex Healthcare LLC registration.
Follow up with Justin Raisun on X9 PKI enablement — promised EOB June 17.
Monitor via DigiCert PKI monitor cron (already active).
tasks.why: SSL/PKI cert required for mTLS PCC API production integration. Blocking the PCC API production path.
